channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Biden to deliver eulogy during Ethel Kennedy memorial service in DC
item.description: Ethel Kennedy, 96, died last week. She had been hospitalized after suffering a stroke earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Las Vegas-area Democratic official sentenced to 28 years for killing reporter in 2022
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A former Las Vegas-area Democratic elected official was sentenced Wednesday to serve at least 28 years in Nevada state prison for killing an investigative journalist who wrote articles critical of his conduct in office two years ago and exposed an intimate relationship with a female
item.dc:creator: KEN RITTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane fallout threatens to hinder voting
item.description: The fallout from a pair of deadly hurricanes threatens to roil voting in the Southeast with complications for election officials and residents alike in states key to this year’s presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Clint Eastwood's daughter arrested in Beverly Hills in domestic violence case
item.description: Actor Francesca Eastwood, daughter of legendary actor Clint Eastwood, was arrested in Beverly Hills over the weekend on suspicion of felony domestic violence, according to reporting from the Los Angeles Times.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KREX 5

item.title: The Latest: Harris and Trump campaigns pivot to turnout as early voting begins
item.description: With just 21 days to go before the final votes are cast in the 2024 presidential season, Kamala Harris and Donald Trump are scrambling to win over and turn out Black voters, women and other key constituencies in what looks to be a razor-tight election.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Navy: Fighter jet with two on board crashed in Washington state during training
item.description: The U.S. Navy was searching for two aviators who went missing after their plane crashed during a routine training flight in Washington state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 102 arrested along Jordan River Trail in 30-day police operation
item.description: The Salt Lake City Police Department announced that 102 people were arrested in a 30-day "enforcement operation.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, NewsNation

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: Nebraskans with felony convictions may register to vote after the state's highest court ruled Wednesday that a top election official had no authority to deem a law restoring those rights unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FTC announces rule to make it easier to cancel subscriptions
item.description: The department will soon implement a "final click to cancel rule," a move that will require sellers to make it "as easy for consumers to cancel their enrollment as it was to sign up.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Iowa emerges as key battleground in race for the House
item.description: In their fight to flip the House, Democrats are eyeing gains in what may seem an unlikely spot: Iowa. Two GOP seats in the Hawkeye State have emerged as true battlegrounds in the final stretch of the campaign, as some election forecasters have moved Reps.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Amazon, Google make dueling nuclear investments to power data centers with clean energy
item.description: Amazon on Wednesday said that it was investing in small nuclear reactors, coming just two days after a similar announcement by Google, as both tech giants seek new sources of carbon-free electricity to meet surging demand from data centers and artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Do you keep money in payment apps? Here's why you probably shouldn't
item.description: As the use of payment apps has grown in recent years, the Consumer Finance Protection Bureau has issued guidance on best practices to avoid pitfalls.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Activists in rural Georgia seek more robust effort to get Black voters to the polls
item.description: DAWSON, Ga. (AP) — The Rev. Ezekiel Holley was in his early 20s when white supremacists burned down two Baptist churches in Terrell County, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Applebee's in Cranston deemed total loss after massive fire
item.description: Firefighters are working to extinguish a large fire that broke out at an Applebee's in Garden City in Cranston.
item.dc:creator: Melanie DaSilva, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans grapple with storm of misinformation from their own party
item.description: Republicans representing states hit by hurricanes Helene and Milton are grappling with the spread of storm misinformation, much of which has stemmed from prominent figures in their own party — including sitting lawmakers and former President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: E. coli found in Laredo's water system
item.description: Residents in the South Texas border city of Laredo for days have had to boil water before using it after dangerous E.coli was found in the city's water system.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Delphi killings trial: State seeks to nix composite sketches
item.description: The motion contended that the “prejudicial effect” of admitting the sketches would far outweigh their “value” in court.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: Pixel Watch 3: Bigger screen, smarter features
item.description: Google Pixel Watch 3 is going big this year! This time around, there's a new option for a larger screen size—45 millimeters. It feels much roomier than the old screen size, which is still available.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Lyft launches voter transportation initiative
item.description: Ride-hailing service Lyft announced that it will offer discounted rideshare, bike-share and scooters on Election Day, an initiative designed to increase transportation access for voters, according to a press release.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: Menendez brothers' family to push for their release as prosecutors review 1989 case
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The extended family of Erik and Lyle Menendez will advocate for the brothers' release from prison following the 1989 killings of their parents in Beverly Hills.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Brightest supermoon rising this week, and it won't be alone
item.description: Get ready to look up for the year’s biggest and brightest supermoon. And it's pairing up with a comet for a rare stargazing two-for-one.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, KRON4

item.title: Former Ozzy Osbourne guitarist Jake E. Lee shot while walking dog in Las Vegas
item.description: Guitarist Jake E. Lee, who formerly played for Ozzy Osbourne, was shot Tuesday while walking his dog, his publicist said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, Fox 5

item.title: Harris and Trump make separate pitches to voters on FOX News and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jets acquire Raiders wide receiver Davante Adams, AP sources say
item.description: The New York Jets have agreed to terms to acquire disgruntled wide receiver Davante Adams from the Las Vegas Raiders, reuniting him with quarterback Aaron Rodgers, two people with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: FEMA temporarily pauses aid in parts of NC over reported threats
item.description: The Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) temporarily paused aid in parts of North Carolina this weekend after alleged threats targeted the agency’s personnel aiding in recovery efforts following Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Tom Donohue, former longtime Chamber of Commerce CEO, dies
item.description: Tom Donohue, who led the U.S. Chamber of Commerce for more than two decades before retiring in 2021, has died, the business lobbying giant announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, KRON4

item.title: Lilly Ledbetter, equal pay trailblazer, dies at 86
item.description: Lilly Ledbetter, an activist for equal pay whose legal fight against her employer paved the way for the Fair Pay Act, has died. She was 86.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Country singer pauses concert as wife gives birth on tour bus
item.description: Country singer Brantley Gilbert's wife, Amber, welcomed their third child on a tour bus in the middle of his concert on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Group tied to polygamous sect builds fences on US Forest Service land
item.description: A group in Colorado has fenced a parcel of U.S. Forest Service Land in an attempt to claim the land under the Homestead Act of 1862. According to the Montezuma County Sheriff's Office, the actions have resulted in a property dispute between the Free Land Holders and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, KTLA 5

item.title: Major home improvement retailer files for bankruptcy, announces sale plans
item.description: True Value, a well-known home improvement company, entered into a sale agreement early Monday morning after filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: List: Here are the nearly 500 Big Lots stores that have or will be closed this year
item.description: Since filing for bankruptcy protection earlier this year, Big Lots has slated nearly 500 stores for closure - some of which have already closed their doors for good.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Trump suggests using National Guard, military against 'enemy from within'
item.description: Former President Donald Trump in a Sunday interview suggested using the National Guard or the military on Election Day to combat what he described as potential chaos from “the enemy from within” – a group that Trump said includes “radical left lunatics.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Billy Crystal and Spike Lee take their places at the Hall of Fame as basketball superfans
item.description: Honored for his devotion to a basketball team that doesn't have a Hall of Fame history, Billy Crystal couldn't help but note the irony. The actor is being added to the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame’s James F.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AP Top 25: Oregon, Penn State move behind No. 1 Texas. Army, Navy both ranked for 1st time since '60
item.description: Oregon and Penn State each moved up a spot in The Associated Press college football poll on Sunday following thrilling wins in high-profile games, and Top 25 newcomers Navy and Army are in the rankings together for the first time since 1960.
item.dc:creator: ERIC OLSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump calls for protester to 'go back home to Mommy' during California rally
item.description: Trump called for the protester to “get the hell knocked out of her,” his latest instance of using violent language when confronted by demonstrators.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Family desperately trying to get Colorado dad home after he fell ill on European cruise
item.description: A Colorado family is desperately trying to get their dad from Englewood home after a sudden and severe illness in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Kerridge, Fox 5

item.title: Mayorkas: Hurricane misinformation 'is extremely pernicious'
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas on Sunday described the flurry of misinformation online about the hurricanes devastating parts of the U.S. as "extremely pernicious," warning that it’s hindering efforts to assist those affected by the storms.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Scully, WGN 9
